chunks.text: Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, BENCH AT SUKKUR Const. Petition No.S-198 of 2025 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge. Hearing of Case 1.For orders on office objections 2.For hearing of main case 12-08-2025 Mr. Alam Sher Khan Bozdar, Advocate along with petitioner. Mr. Saeed Ahmed Wassan, Assistant A.G a/w SIP Abdul Ghafoor Mojai of P.S, Mirpur Mathelo and SIP Hassan Ali of P.S, Belo Mirpur. **** By means of the instant petition, the petitioner has alleged that he is being harassed by the official respondents at the behest of private respondents. He has prayed that the official respondents be directed not to harass him and that protection be provided to him and his family members. Today, learned AAG files statements on behalf of respondents No.2 to 5, which are taken on record; copies whereof are provided to learned counsel for the petitioner. He submits that both the petitioner and the private respondents are in a longstanding dispute over landed property, and that the official respondents have neither caused any sort of harassment to the petitioner, as alleged, nor intend to do so in future. However, due protection will be provided to the petitioner in accordance with law. After such statement, learned counsel for the petitioner, being satisfied, seeks disposal of the instant petition. Accordingly, the instant petition stands disposed of with directions to the official respondents to conduct themselves strictly in accordance with law and to provide legal protection to the petitioner. However, it is made clear that this order shall not come in the way of any criminal, civil, or family proceedings, if pending between the parties before the competent Courts of law.

Accordingly, the instant petition stands disposed of with directions to the official respondents to conduct themselves strictly in accordance with law and to provide legal protection to the petitioner. However, it is made clear that this order shall not come in the way of any criminal, civil, or family proceedings, if pending between the parties before the competent Courts of law. JUDGE Ahmad
